input.headline: Wales, Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland find out World Cup play-off opponents

raw.headline: Wales, Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland find out World Cup play-off opponents

winner: The teams were thrilled until they realized the World Cup play-off was just a game of Twister with the UK as the center.

candidates.text: Why did the Irish soccer team bring a map to the World Cup draw? So they wouldn't get *lost in translation*—especially between Wales and Northern Ireland!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the context of the World Cup draw involving Wales, Northern Ireland, and the Republic of Ireland. The pun on 'lost in translation' cleverly ties into both geography and language, making it both natural and funny."}

candidates.text: What's the problem with Wales playing a World Cup play-off? They'll just end up in Northern Ireland... or Ireland.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'Northern Ireland' and 'Ireland,' but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about the World Cup context. The humor is mild and relies on a pun that may not land as strongly for all audiences."}

candidates.text: Wales, Northern Ireland, and the Republic of Ireland gathered around a table, nervously flipping through a deck of cards labeled "World Cup Play-Off Opponents," hoping none of them would be the one with the image of their own face on it.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific scenario with a surprising twist, effectively using the context of the headline."}

candidates.text: Wales, Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland find out their World Cup play-off opponents — turns out, they're all still waiting on the same match result.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the situation with a concise and specific punchline that highlights the shared waiting experience humorously."}

candidates.text: Ireland’s soccer team got so excited about their World Cup play-off opponent that they tried to kick the referee—turns out he was just a neutral umpire from Scotland.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but it misrepresents the context (referees are not umpires in soccer). It's also slightly generic in its setup and lacks full alignment with the headline."}

candidates.text: The teams were thrilled until they realized the World Cup play-off was just a game of Twister with the UK as the center.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist involving Twister and the UK as the center. However, it lacks some depth and could be more directly tied to the teams mentioned."}

candidates.text: The Irish and Welsh teams are nervous about the play-offs, but they’re hoping for a result that puts them on the map instead of just on the pitch.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'on the map,' but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about the teams or the context of the World Cup play-offs."}

candidates.text: The three nations finally agreed to join forces and play together, only to realize they were actually just being drafted to share a single locker room.
The three nations were so distressed by the news that they collectively decided to move into a single house and share a bathroom, just to ensure they wouldn't have to face any opponents alone.
